status: Five-seed v1.1.1 bridge-isolation pilot completed. Protocol-integrity gates passed. Route-swap ordering and prediction-identity preservation passed. Held-out nominal mediation, held-out functional mediation, local continuity, functional causal specificity, and operational non-degradation did not qualify.
supported_claims: The sensory encoder, context encoder, common host bank, synthesis layer, classifier, and common functional cost matrix were held fixed across router treatments.
Router forward-image and optimizer-step budgets were matched within every model seed.
Far-factor route swaps produced larger common-cost functional changes than near-factor route swaps.
Controlled route interventions preserved prediction identity under the preregistered gate.
partial_findings: R3 produced positive mean held-out nominal and functional order effects, but seed intervals crossed zero.
R2 and R3 reduced held-out functional stress, while prototype-dominated routing created heavy local-continuity tails.
The frozen ecosystem carries non-arbitrary route-function structure, but the tested hybrid router did not exploit it smoothly or operationally.
failed_or_unqualified_claims: held-out functional route mediation qualification under the common cost matrix
operational non-degradation within the one-point margin
correction_note: The original notebook export retained a pre-execution status string. This corrected manifest changes metadata only; CSV, checkpoint, and trace evidence are unchanged.
